title: BuildInfo
description: Self-contained, parameterized artifact fully specifying how to reproduce ONE alkane's on-chain wasm from source, byte-for-byte, in a controlled sandbox. Covers every environment axis found to matter for a reproducible alkane build.

schema_version.description: Semver; consumers accept any artifact of the same MAJOR (1.x). Current spec: 1.2.0. Lineage in schema.rs SCHEMA_VERSION docs.

cargo_lock_b64.description: base64 of the exact Cargo.lock used (ground truth).

target_source.description: onchain = fetched by alkane id via jsonrpc getbytecode; local-file = a .wasm on disk (offline; also the path for not-yet-deployed alkanes).

cloned_from.description: For a clone: the template id/URL whose bytecode getbytecode resolved to; verifying the template transitively verifies this alkane.

subdir.description: Sub-directory (workspace-member path) the crate lives in.

content.description: Raw UTF-8, or base64:<...> for binary.

rustc_version.description: (1.1.0) semver x.y.z OR a channel (nightly-YYYY-MM-DD / beta) — the builder rustup-installs it and omits a channel string from Cargo.toml rust-version.

host_triple.description: Toolchain dir name whose std panic paths must match, e.g. 1.86.0-aarch64-apple-darwin. THE macOS TRICK: physically COPY (not symlink — rustc canonicalizes) the linux toolchain to $RUSTUP_HOME/toolchains/<host_triple> so embedded .../toolchains/<host_triple>/... paths match.

RustcSource.description: (1.2.0) How rustc is obtained. Absent ⇒ rustup installs rustc_version (channel/semver). method=git-build compiles rustc from a rust-lang/rust commit — the only way to reproduce a contract built off an UNPUBLISHED toolchain (e.g. a stable branch-point whose /rustc/<hash>/ sysroot no rustup channel ships). Parallel to CToolchain.source.

git_commit.description: git-build: rust-lang/rust commit to compile; equals Toolchain.rustc_commit (the /rustc/<hash>/ sysroot hash).

git_repo.description: git-build: source repo when not canonical rust-lang/rust.

channel.description: git-build: release channel to configure. Affects CODEGEN (debug-assertions / overflow-checks defaults), not just the version string — the wrong channel diverges output even at the right commit.

download_ci_llvm.description: git-build: use the commit's CI-built LLVM so codegen matches the official release build. false ⇒ build the bundled src/llvm-project (slower, may diverge). Defaults true.

CToolchain.description: Present only when the crate links C (e.g. secp256k1-sys). vendor+version are recorded verbatim in the wasm producers section, so they must match exactly.

c_object_host_dependent.description: The secp256k1 C-object host-dependence: a macOS-built and a Linux-built Homebrew clang of the SAME LLVM version emit a marginally different secp256k1 static footprint, shifting the memory base a few bytes. When true, a Linux reconstruction reaches `verified` (logic + producers exact) but not full `reproducible` without the origin host clang.

wasm_cflags.description: (1.1.0) Extra CFLAGS for the wasm C compile, e.g. -nostdlibinc — drops the host glibc /usr/include leak on wasm32-unknown-unknown so a stock apt clang can cross-compile secp256k1→wasm (keeping clang builtins + the crate's bundled wasm-sysroot). Empty ⇒ a self-contained (Homebrew) clang.

method.description: How the wasm C compiler is obtained. (1.2.0) `wasi-sdk` = the clang shipped in a WASI SDK release (e.g. wasi-sdk-20's bin/clang, which reports `clang 16.0.0`); `cc` retargets it from wasm32-wasi to wasm32-unknown-unknown. Used by e.g. kungfuflex/predicates.

homebrew_bottles.description: GHCR bottle blob sha256s to assemble Homebrew clang on Linux (llvm+z3+libedit), then patchelf the interpreter/rpath.

home.description: Builder HOME reversed from embedded panic paths; registry/git-checkout/toolchain paths hang off this.

remap_path_prefix.description: --remap-path-prefix rules; empty ⇒ paths are raw and HOME must be reconstructed.

mode.description: committed-lock = repo commits Cargo.lock; use real crates.io + --locked. time-machine = no committed lock; freeze the crates.io-index tree at the build date and serve it AS index.crates.io so the registry src-dir hash is canonical for that cargo version.

archive_commit.description: rust-lang/crates.io-index-archive commit frozen to the build date (time-machine). (1.1.0) OPTIONAL — when absent it is auto-derived from `timestamp` (the deploy date): pick the snapshot-YYYY-MM-DD branch that CONTAINS that date (smallest snapshot ≥ date) and rev-list --before.

registry_hash.description: Canonical index.crates.io-<hash> src-dir cargo produces (cargo-version dependent).

source_form.description: bare = resolve WITH the rev then rewrite the lockfile source-id to bare (?rev=…# → #) and build --locked, so -C metadata (hence monomorphization order) matches a bare-git-URL build. rev = leave the ?rev= source-id.

redirect_to.description: (1.1.0) Unify a MIRRORED repo: rewrite every git="url" for THIS url to redirect_to (same crate, different host, e.g. sandshrewmetaprotocols↔kungfuflex metashrew) so cargo doesn't build two incompatible copies.

pin_transitive.description: (1.1.0) Force rev onto ALL lockfile entries from this git source incl. transitive ones (cargo update -p <crate> --precise <rev>), not just the in-tree Cargo.toml.

verdict.description: reproducible = byte-exact sha256; verified = code+structure exact, only build-metadata/host-artifact bytes differ; code_match = code section identical; partial = ≥98% normalized; mismatch = below threshold.
